name: Trilocanasiva
dates_notes: Placed in twelfth-century South India in the Sanskrit Śaiva Siddhānta digest and commentary tradition.
core_thesis: Trilocanaśiva is a twelfth-century South Indian Śaiva Siddhānta author associated with doctrinal and ritual digests. The gap report identifies the *Prāyaścittasamuccaya*, *Siddhāntasamuccaya*, and *Siddhāntasārāvalī* as the relevant attested works. His place in the roster is as a Sanskrit Siddhānta systematizer rather than as a founder: the works gather and order doctrine, expiation, and Siddhānta categories for scholastic and ritual use. Because the cited editions are point/critical-edition availability rather than clean free text, this entry keeps all passage fields empty.

engaged_works.title: Prayascittasamuccaya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Identified in the Śaiva Siddhānta digest tradition and modern critical-edition record as Trilocanaśiva's work.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit Śaiva Siddhānta digest concerned with expiation and ritual correction. It belongs to the same scholastic-ritual environment in which doctrine and practice are treated as integrated rather than separate. No clean local text has been acquired.

engaged_works.title: Siddhantasamuccaya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Attested as a Trilocanaśiva work in the Sanskrit Śaiva Siddhānta digest tradition.
engaged_works.summary: A Siddhānta compendium that gathers doctrinal materials for scholastic use. Its relevance is as evidence of the South Indian Sanskrit Śaiva Siddhānta digest tradition. No clean local text has been acquired.

engaged_works.title: Siddhantasaravali
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Attested as a Trilocanaśiva work in the Śaiva Siddhānta scholastic record.
engaged_works.summary: A concise Śaiva Siddhānta summary work. It records Siddhānta categories in digest form and is included here as part of Trilocanaśiva's attested philosophical and ritual corpus. No clean local text has been acquired.
